root_text.sn55.27:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena anāthapiṇḍiko gahapati ābādhiko hoti dukkhito bāḷhagilāno.
root_text.sn55.27:1.3: Atha kho anāthapiṇḍiko gahapati aññataraṁ purisaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.sn55.27:1.4: “ehi tvaṁ, ambho purisa, yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkama; upasaṅkamitvā mama vacanena āyasmato ānandassa pāde sirasā vanda:
root_text.sn55.27:1.6: So āyasmato ānandassa pāde sirasā vandatī’ti.
root_text.sn55.27:1.8: ‘sādhu kira, bhante, āyasmā ānando yena anāthapiṇḍikassa gahapatissa nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkamatu anukampaṁ upādāyā’”ti.
root_text.sn55.27:2.1: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho so puriso anāthapiṇḍikassa gahapatissa paṭissutvā yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so puriso āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn55.27:2.3: So āyasmato ānandassa pāde sirasā vandati.
root_text.sn55.27:2.5: ‘sādhu kira, bhante, āyasmā ānando yena anāthapiṇḍikassa gahapatissa nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkamatu anukampaṁ upādāyā’”ti.
root_text.sn55.27:3.1: Atha kho āyasmā ānando pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena anāthapiṇḍikassa gahapatissa nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi. Nisajja kho āyasmā ānando anāthapiṇḍikaṁ gahapatiṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn55.27:3.2: “kacci te, gahapati, khamanīyaṁ, kacci yāpanīyaṁ? Kacci dukkhā vedanā paṭikkamanti, no abhikkamanti; paṭikkamosānaṁ paññāyati, no abhikkamo”ti?
root_text.sn55.27:3.3: “Na me, bhante, khamanīyaṁ na yāpanīyaṁ. Bāḷhā me dukkhā vedanā abhikkamanti, no paṭikkamanti; abhikkamosānaṁ paññāyati, no paṭikkamo”ti.
root_text.sn55.27:4.1: “Catūhi kho, gahapati, dhammehi samannāgatassa assutavato puthujjanassa hoti uttāso, hoti chambhitattaṁ, hoti samparāyikaṁ maraṇabhayaṁ.
root_text.sn55.27:4.3: Idha, gahapati, assutavā puthujjano buddhe appasādena samannāgato hoti.
root_text.sn55.27:4.4: Tañca panassa buddhe appasādaṁ attani samanupassato hoti uttāso, hoti chambhitattaṁ, hoti samparāyikaṁ maraṇabhayaṁ.
root_text.sn55.27:5.1: Puna caparaṁ, gahapati, assutavā puthujjano dhamme appasādena samannāgato hoti.
root_text.sn55.27:5.2: Tañca panassa dhamme appasādaṁ attani samanupassato hoti uttāso, hoti chambhitattaṁ, hoti samparāyikaṁ maraṇabhayaṁ.
root_text.sn55.27:6.1: Puna caparaṁ, gahapati, assutavā puthujjano saṅghe appasādena samannāgato hoti.
root_text.sn55.27:6.2: Tañca panassa saṅghe appasādaṁ attani samanupassato hoti uttāso, hoti chambhitattaṁ, hoti samparāyikaṁ maraṇabhayaṁ.
root_text.sn55.27:7.1: Puna caparaṁ, gahapati, assutavā puthujjano dussīlyena samannāgato hoti.
root_text.sn55.27:7.2: Tañca panassa dussīlyaṁ attani samanupassato hoti uttāso, hoti chambhitattaṁ, hoti samparāyikaṁ maraṇabhayaṁ.
root_text.sn55.27:7.3: Imehi kho, gahapati, catūhi dhammehi samannāgatassa assutavato puthujjanassa hoti uttāso, hoti chambhitattaṁ, hoti samparāyikaṁ maraṇabhayaṁ.
root_text.sn55.27:8.1: Catūhi kho, gahapati, dhammehi samannāgatassa sutavato ariyasāvakassa na hoti uttāso, na hoti chambhitattaṁ, na hoti samparāyikaṁ maraṇabhayaṁ.
root_text.sn55.27:8.3: Idha, gahapati, sutavā ariyasāvako buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti—
root_text.sn55.27:8.4: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.27:8.5: Tañca panassa buddhe aveccappasādaṁ attani samanupassato na hoti uttāso, na hoti chambhitattaṁ, na hoti samparāyikaṁ maraṇabhayaṁ.
root_text.sn55.27:9.1: Puna caparaṁ, gahapati, sutavā ariyasāvako dhamme aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti—
root_text.sn55.27:9.2: svākkhāto bhagavatā dhammo …pe… paccattaṁ veditabbo viññūhīti.
root_text.sn55.27:9.3: Tañca panassa dhamme aveccappasādaṁ attani samanupassato na hoti uttāso, na hoti chambhitattaṁ, na hoti samparāyikaṁ maraṇabhayaṁ.
root_text.sn55.27:10.1: Puna caparaṁ, gahapati, sutavā ariyasāvako saṅghe aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti—
root_text.sn55.27:10.2: suppaṭipanno bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho …pe… anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassāti.
root_text.sn55.27:10.3: Tañca panassa saṅghe aveccappasādaṁ attani samanupassato na hoti uttāso, na hoti chambhitattaṁ, na hoti samparāyikaṁ maraṇabhayaṁ.
root_text.sn55.27:11.1: Puna caparaṁ, gahapati, sutavā ariyasāvako ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgato hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
root_text.sn55.27:11.2: Tāni ca panassa ariyakantāni sīlāni attani samanupassato na hoti uttāso, na hoti chambhitattaṁ, na hoti samparāyikaṁ maraṇabhayaṁ.
root_text.sn55.27:11.3: Imehi kho, gahapati, catūhi dhammehi samannāgatassa sutavato ariyasāvakassa na hoti uttāso, na hoti chambhitattaṁ, na hoti samparāyikaṁ maraṇabhayan”ti.
root_text.sn55.27:12.3: Ahañhi, bhante, buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato homi—
root_text.sn55.27:12.4: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.27:12.7: suppaṭipanno bhagavato sāvakasaṅgho …pe… anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassāti.
root_text.sn55.27:12.8: Yāni cimāni, bhante, bhagavatā gihisāmīcikāni sikkhāpadāni desitāni, nāhaṁ tesaṁ kiñci attani khaṇḍaṁ samanupassāmī”ti.

translation_text.sn55.27:1.2: Now at that time the householder Anāthapiṇḍika was sick, suffering, gravely ill.
translation_text.sn55.27:1.4: “Please, my friend, go to Venerable Ānanda, and in my name bow with your head to his feet. Say to him:
translation_text.sn55.27:1.5: ‘Sir, the householder Anāthapiṇḍika is sick, suffering, gravely ill.
translation_text.sn55.27:1.6: He bows with his head to your feet.’
translation_text.sn55.27:1.8: ‘Sir, please visit him at his home out of sympathy.’”
translation_text.sn55.27:2.1: “Yes, sir,” that man replied. He did as Anāthapiṇḍika asked.
translation_text.sn55.27:3.1: Then Venerable Ānanda robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, went to the home of the householder Anāthapiṇḍika. He sat down on the seat spread out and said to Anāthapiṇḍika,
translation_text.sn55.27:3.2: “I hope you’re keeping well, householder; I hope you’re all right. And I hope the pain is fading, not growing; that its fading, not its growing, is apparent.”
translation_text.sn55.27:3.3: “Sir, I’m not keeping well, I’m not getting by. The pain is terrible and growing, not fading; its growing is evident, not its fading.”
translation_text.sn55.27:4.1: “Householder, when an unlearned ordinary person has four things, they’re frightened and terrified, and fear what awaits them after death.
translation_text.sn55.27:4.3: Firstly, an unlearned ordinary person distrusts the Buddha.
translation_text.sn55.27:4.4: Seeing in themselves that distrust of the Buddha, they’re frightened and terrified, and fear what awaits them after death.
translation_text.sn55.27:5.1: Furthermore, an unlearned ordinary person distrusts the teaching …
translation_text.sn55.27:6.1: Furthermore, an unlearned ordinary person distrusts the Saṅgha …
translation_text.sn55.27:7.1: Furthermore, an unlearned ordinary person has unethical conduct.
translation_text.sn55.27:7.2: Seeing in themselves that unethical conduct, they’re frightened and terrified, and fear what awaits them after death.
translation_text.sn55.27:7.3: When an unlearned ordinary person has these four things, they’re frightened and terrified, and fear what awaits them after death.
translation_text.sn55.27:8.1: When a learned noble disciple has four things, they’re not frightened or terrified, and don’t fear what awaits them after death.
translation_text.sn55.27:8.3: Firstly, a noble disciple has experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.27:8.5: Seeing in themselves that experiential confidence in the Buddha, they’re not frightened or terrified, and don’t fear what awaits them after death.
translation_text.sn55.27:9.1: Furthermore, a noble disciple has experiential confidence in the teaching …
translation_text.sn55.27:10.1: Furthermore, a noble disciple has experiential confidence in the Saṅgha …
translation_text.sn55.27:11.1: Furthermore, a noble disciple’s ethical conduct is loved by the noble ones, unbroken, impeccable, spotless, and unmarred, liberating, praised by sensible people, not mistaken, and leading to immersion.
translation_text.sn55.27:11.2: Seeing in themselves that ethical conduct loved by the noble ones, they’re not frightened or terrified, and don’t fear what awaits them after death.
translation_text.sn55.27:11.3: When a learned noble disciple has these four things, they’re not frightened or terrified, and don’t fear what awaits them after death.”
translation_text.sn55.27:12.3: For I have experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.27:12.8: And of the training rules appropriate for laypeople taught by the Buddha, I don’t see any that I have broken.”
translation_text.sn55.27:12.10: You have declared the fruit of stream-entry.”
